rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,2-dihalogenation (F, Cl, Br) of gem-difluoroalkenes using HX (HF, HCl, HBr) as halide source to deliver CF2X-CHX-R type products including 18F-labeled tracers
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide (X-) from HX to halogen radical/halogen species which adds to the gem-difluoroalkene; the resulting carbon radical/cation is trapped by a second halide

key_intermediates.name: halide-derived halogen radical (X•) from anodic oxidation of HX
β-fluorinated carbon radical from X• addition to gem-difluoroalkene (CF2X-C•(H)-R)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes X- (from HX) to a halogen radical that adds to the terminal CF2=CHR alkene to give a CF2X-substituted carbon radical; this radical is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation and trapped by a second halide to yield the vicinal dihalide.

probe_reagents.name: K[18F]F-K222 (Kryptofix-complexed [18F]fluoride)
probe_reagents.observation: Enables 18F-labeling of the dihalogenated product, supporting halide incorporation pathway and giving 18F-labeled tracers

notes: Only the introductory/abstract page was provided; the detailed mechanistic scheme (radical traps, CV, DFT) is not shown here. Mechanism inferred from Scheme 1b (HX + electrolysis, classification as Method III electrochemical dihalogenation) and analogy to prior electrochemical olefin dihalogenations cited in the introduction.
